title: Darren Aronofsky on Pushing the Envelope | The Big Picture (Ep. 28)
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Darren Aronofsky a few weeks after mother! split critics and drew an F CinemaScore, about why he chases intensity and grounds every provocation in something real.

films.title: mother!
films.note: The film in the room. Jennifer Lawrence as a stand-in for Mother Earth, Javier Bardem as a god-like creator, Ed Harris and Michelle Pfeiffer as Adam and Eve. Written fast, then rehearsed for weeks in a taped-out warehouse in Brooklyn while the house was built in Montreal.

films.title: Black Swan
films.note: Aronofsky on the cracked-toenail moment as a jump scare built out of realism; also his example of a film that tested awfully and got made anyway.

films.title: Requiem for a Dream
films.note: The MPAA wanted the intense ending trimmed; cutting it, he says, would have undermined the whole point about how far addicts go.

films.title: Noah
films.note: The Old Testament immersion he says fed mother!; its box-office success is what let Paramount greenlight this one.

films.title: The Wrestler
films.note: "No one wants to see a feature with Mickey Rourke": his example of getting a risky film made by finding the right people at the right price.

films.title: Pi
films.note: Almost 20 years since he started shooting it; he gets the rights back at the 20-year mark, a Jim Jarmusch habit he admired.

films.title: She's Gotta Have It
films.note: The Spike Lee film he caught on the one open seat in a Brooklyn mall, his first independent film and the turn toward the alternative.

films.title: Barton Fink
films.note: Flipping channels, it came on and re-inspired him: his answer for how he decides what to do next.

films.title: The Tribe
films.note: The Ukrainian film he cites as a recent reminder of the power of cinema.

films.title: The Meyerowitz Stories (New and Selected)
films.note: Teased at the close: next week's guest is Noah Baumbach on his Netflix film.
